quoteEvidence.acceptance: Accepted quote fields equal the corresponding fields parsed from the current source at execution time; no frozen quote value or clock is acceptance truth.

dailyBarEvidence.acceptance: Accepted bar cutoff, retrieval time, and row count equal the corresponding fields parsed from the current source at execution time; technical expected values derive from the parsed rows.

planningConstraints.credentialBoundary: Cache-first first view requires no credential. Optional quote refresh remains secondary and uses only centralized RLDATA/RLAPP policy with index.html#data-settings; no page-local credential surface, store, migration, tokenized URL, or direct provider transport is authorized.

scenarios.title: Cache-First First View

gherkin.given:
the static fundamental model is identified as of 2026-07-06
And the committed MSFT quote and daily-bar cache envelopes are valid
gherkin.when: the production page boots without a provider credential or Fetch action
gherkin.then:
the static model paints before both cache reads settle
And each market resource is independently accepted through production validators
And no provider request occurs
And the model cutoff remains 2026-07-06

linkedTests: tests/msft-july-market-refresh.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-009-001/002/005 cache-first market truth
tests/msft-july-market-refresh.spec.mjs

scenarios.title: Separate Market And Model Clocks

gherkin.given:
the accepted quote carries its provider token and retrieval instant
And the accepted daily bars carry their own cutoff and retrieval instant
gherkin.then:
model as-of, quote provider as-of, quote retrieval, bar cutoff, bar retrieval, and evaluation time occupy separate fields
And no page-wide live or ambiguous data_as_of value represents them
And the timezone-less provider token is preserved verbatim

linkedTests: tests/msft-july-market-refresh.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-009-001/002/005 cache-first market truth
tests/msft-july-market-refresh.spec.mjs

scenarios.title: Spot Reprices The Existing Scenario Only

gherkin.given: every active scenario input is snapshotted before quote acceptance
gherkin.when: production acceptance commits a valid delayed spot
gherkin.then:
spot over modeled FY27E EPS and scenario price versus spot recompute
And every scenario input remains byte-equivalent
And selected scenario P/E remains the user's value
And the market multiple is labeled model-relative rather than consensus forward P/E

scenarios.title: User Edits Survive Market Outcomes

gherkin.given: the user changed Q4 revenue, FY27 depreciation, and selected P/E
gherkin.when: quote acceptance, rejection, or refresh failure settles
gherkin.then:
the exact edited values remain selected and persisted
And only quote-owned state and spot-dependent projections may differ
And model date and daily technical state remain unchanged

scenarios.title: Daily Technicals Use Daily Rows Only

gherkin.given:
the committed daily cache has enough valid ordered rows
And the accepted delayed quote has a different value
gherkin.then:
close, SMA20, SMA50, SMA200, High252, stack, and signed distances derive only from the daily rows
And no synthetic row is added from the delayed quote
And the technical cutoff remains the last eligible daily-bar date

scenarios.title: Quote Missing Produces Bars-Only Truth

gherkin.given:
the static model can render
And the accepted daily bars support technical calculations
gherkin.when: the quote outcome is missing or rejected
gherkin.then:
market status is partial
And quote-owned and spot-dependent fields are unavailable
And daily technicals retain their own cutoff
And no old hard-coded spot is presented as current

scenarios.title: Bars Missing Produces Quote-Only Truth

gherkin.given: the accepted quote has valid value and timestamps
gherkin.when: the bars outcome is missing or rejected
gherkin.then:
market status is partial
And spot-dependent model comparisons remain available
And every unsupported daily technical field is unavailable with a scoped reason
And no default trend or moving average is inserted

scenarios.title: Stale And Malformed Inputs Stay Isolated

gherkin.given:
the quote is valid but older than quote policy
And the bars candidate has invalid symbol, metadata, rows, ordering, or cutoff
gherkin.when: production validators and reducers process both outcomes
gherkin.then:
the quote remains visible with stale status and original clocks
And the bars candidate is rejected with its stable reason code
And the model and independently valid state remain visible without neutral substitutes or a crash

scenarios.title: Central Optional Refresh Preserves Cache Truth

gherkin.given:
the cache-first view is already useful
And central policy reports Finnhub disabled or unconfigured
gherkin.then:
no direct provider request or page-local credential operation occurs
And the cache-backed accepted state remains visible
And the scoped status explains the central reason
And an unconfigured credential links to index.html#data-settings

scenarios.title: Failed And Out-Of-Order Attempts Preserve Accepted Truth

gherkin.given: request sequence 2 has produced an accepted quote
gherkin.when:
request sequence 1 completes with an older candidate
And a subsequent request fails
gherkin.then:
request sequence 2 remains accepted
And failure is recorded without clearing the accepted quote
And bars, model inputs, selected P/E, and the 2026-07-06 model cutoff remain unchanged

both modes show the same spot, technical conclusion, valuation, and clocks
And no request or scenario mutation occurs
And Power canvases redraw nonblank with adjacent textual or tabular equivalents

scenarios.title: Static-Model Tool Read Retains Its Boundary

gherkin.given: the user may have edited active scenario controls
gherkin.when: the production static tool-read builder publishes through RLDATA
gherkin.then:
it uses committed Base assumptions and accepted source-qualified market evidence
And activeUserScenarioIncluded, recommendationEligible, and marketAggregationEligible are false
And model as-of remains 2026-07-06
And no FY26 Q4 actual, current consensus, credential, raw option contract, or raw bar row is published

only the existing MSFT records and marker-bounded Feature 009 test group differ for this feature
And tool id, page path, notes path, navigation identity, and static-model profile remain stable
And page labels, CSV, normalized read, notes, and registry descriptions express the same model and market cutoffs
And protected unrelated hunks remain byte-equivalent
